overview: Sneak and Show is a Legacy combo deck that leverages Lotus Petal for rapid mana acceleration to cast Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. The goal is to bypass mana costs and cheat powerful threats or multiple creatures onto the battlefield before opponents can interact. By combining artifact ramp with instant-speed cheating, the deck aims to assemble a game-winning board state on turns two or three.
win_conditions: Casting Show and Tell to cheat a zero-mana-cost threat onto the battlefield
Casting Sneak Attack to flood the board with creatures for an overwhelming attack
Establishing a protected combo engine that outpaces opponent interaction

keep_criteria: Hands containing Lotus Petal paired with Show and Tell or Sneak Attack
Hands with multiple cantrips and at least one combo piece
Hands that can assemble the combo by turn two or three
Keep hands with 1-2 lands plus at least one acceleration source (Lotus Petal, Dark Ritual, Chrome Mox, etc.) and a win condition (Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, or a creature to cheat).
Keep hands with Thoughtseize/Liliana of the Veil if paired with a land or acceleration.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Lotus Petal or other mana acceleration
Hands with only slow, non-combo cards
Hands that cannot cast Show and Tell or Sneak Attack by turn three

Mulligan pure land draws, hands with only threats and no acceleration, hands with multiple copies of the same key spell without mana support, or hands with zero interaction against interactive formats.

early_game: Prioritize playing Lotus Petal to generate excess mana
Draw cards to locate Show and Tell or Sneak Attack
Protect your combo pieces from early disruption
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Lotus Petal/Dark Ritual, activate for mana, cast Show and Tell or Sneak Attack, and put a threat onto the battlefield.
If holding discard, use it on turn 1 to strip key answers or combo pieces.
Turn 2: Often the win turn if turn 1 was disrupted, or continue accelerating/attacking with Sneak Attack creatures.

Sequence lands carefully to ensure you can cast acceleration spells without flooding, and prioritize fetching basic lands if your deck runs duals that require specific colors for protection or interaction.
mid_game: Cast Show and Tell to cheat a high-impact threat onto the battlefield
Use Sneak Attack to put multiple creatures into play simultaneously
Establish board presence that forces opponents to use interaction
Mana is your most critical resource
always prioritize keeping open mana for acceleration and key spells.
Manage hand by using discard early to disrupt, but don't overcommit if you have no win condition.
Graveyard is a resource for recursion (Liliana, Fetchlands) but also a vulnerability
be mindful of graveyard hate and avoid overloading it with dead cards.
Commit threats only when you can protect them or win immediately.
late_game: Leverage cheated-in threats to control the board or generate value
Use Sneak Attack tokens or creatures to push lethal damage
Maintain card advantage to outlast opponent resources

Progenitus or Emrakul for board wipes and protection, Kiki-Jiki, Niavar's Genius with Mirror Image for infinite combat, Thassa's Oracle with Tendrils of Agony for a combo finish, or Sneak Attack creatures attacking twice.
Get there by accelerating into Show and Tell/Sneak Attack, or by setting up the combo pieces in hand/graveyard through discard or recursion.
Go all-in when you have the full combo package in hand/graveyard, when the opponent has no interaction left, or when you can win before they can respond.
Also commit if you are ahead on board and can close out the game, or if the opponent is tapped out for a key answer.

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Play early to enable Show and Tell or Sneak Attack on turn two or three. Protect it from removal to ensure combo speed.

key_cards.name: Show and Tell
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have enough mana to pay its cost. Target a zero-mana-cost creature or artifact that can stabilize the game or win it outright.

key_cards.name: Sneak Attack
key_cards.notes: Hold until you have sufficient mana or when you need to bypass countermagic. Use it to put multiple creatures onto the battlefield for a lethal push or to overwhelm removal.

sequencing_tips: Always play Lotus Petal before casting Show and Tell to minimize mana requirements
Cast Show and Tell before Sneak Attack if you need a single powerful threat to stabilize
Save Sneak Attack for when you can go for lethal or when opponents have exhausted their counterspells
Use card draw spells to find combo pieces before committing to the board

Common mistakes include overcommitting to a single win condition without backup, mulliganing too aggressively (you need acceleration plus win condition), ignoring the opponent's interaction, and failing to sequence lands properly.

Unique interactions include using fetchlands to tutor for lands or shuffle away unwanted cards, and activating Lotus Petal for mana even if you have no spells to cast immediately (though it is dead if not used).
Always be mindful of the stack and priority when casting Show and Tell/Sneak Attack, and practice your turn sequences to ensure you can execute under pressure.

matchups.advice: Race to assemble your combo before they stabilize. Use Sneak Attack to overwhelm their counterspell suite.
Use Show and Tell to cheat in a large threat that can block multiple attackers. Prioritize Lotus Petal for speed.
Go first and execute your combo as quickly as possible. Use interaction if available to delay their win condition.
Establish a board presence with Show and Tell before they develop their threats. Use Sneak Attack to push damage through their blockers.

Use discard to strip counterspells, cast acceleration spells that are hard to counter, sequence Show and Tell/Sneak Attack when they are tapped out, or use uncounterable threats. If they have multiple counters, consider going for a cheaper win condition or using free spells (like Force of Will if in your list) to protect your combo.

Race them with your own acceleration, use discard to strip their mana rocks, or bring in graveyard hate and board wipes. If they are ahead, use counterspells and discard to disrupt their key spells, and look for ways to win before they can cast their massive threats.

Win the resource war by drawing more cards, using discard effectively, and sideboarding in hate for their key threats. Prioritize keeping hands with acceleration and win conditions, and do not overcommit if they have backup interaction. Track their graveyard and hand to anticipate their win condition, and be ready to disrupt it with discard or counters.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Force of Will, Daze, Spell Pierce), removal (Path to Exile, Lightning Bolt), and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for interaction versus when they hold up mana for protection.
Track if they are playing a control shell (holding multiple answers) or a combo shell (going for the win).
Pay attention to whether they are playing fetchlands or shock lands, as this indicates their mana base and potential for quick interaction.
